questions.question: According to Robin Dunbar's research, what is the direct correlation between contact frequency and relationship closeness when distance separates friends?

options.text: Contact frequency has no impact on closeness
Distance reduces frequency ~25% without intentional compensation
Distance reduces frequency ~50% without intentional compensation
Distance reduces frequency ~75% without intentional compensation

questions.explanation: Dunbar's research reveals direct correlation between contact frequency and relationship closeness—close friendships (5 intimate, 15 close) require weekly-to-monthly contact to maintain intimacy. Distance reduces frequency ~50% on average without intentional compensation because casual contact disappears (no running into each other), spontaneity vanishes (every interaction requires scheduling), and coordination complexity increases. Without scheduled rituals and platform variety, friendships naturally drift from inner circles to outer circles even with good intentions.

questions.question: What characterizes 'flexible not fragile' friendships that survive distance?

options.text: Rigid expectations that weekly hangouts must continue or friendship ends
Form changes (daily → monthly) to match circumstances, but bond and core commitment remain—'pick up where we left off' ability
Complete emotional distance to prevent disappointment
Never allowing any gaps in communication

questions.explanation: Flexible friendships adapt form to changing circumstances (daily texts → monthly video calls) while maintaining core commitment, demonstrate patience with gaps ('pause not permanent'), and have 'pick up where we left off' ability even after months between contact. Fragile friendships have rigid expectations (must see each other weekly or 'not real friends'), interpret reduced contact as rejection, refuse to adapt form, and end when circumstances change. Flexible friendships survive life transitions by adjusting communication patterns while core commitment signals (remembering birthdays, checking in during major events, showing up for scheduled calls) demonstrate the bond remains.

questions.question: According to research on long-distance friendship sustainability, how many in-person visits per year minimum are needed to maintain intimate connection?

options.text: No visits needed if video calls are frequent
1-2 visits per year minimum
3-4 visits per year minimum
Monthly visits required

questions.explanation: Research shows long-distance friendships require 1-2 in-person visits per year minimum to maintain intimate connection. Video calls maintain relationships BETWEEN visits but don't replace them—there's no substitute for physical presence and extended time together. Visit quality matters: prioritize unstructured 'just hang out' time over overscheduling, mix activities with downtime, minimize others (focus on friend not their entire social network), and create new memories. Schedule NEXT visit before leaving current one to prevent drift between reunions.

questions.question: What combination of communication platforms provides optimal coverage for different long-distance connection needs?

options.text: Only video calls for everything
Only text messaging to keep it simple
Video for depth (monthly), messaging for frequency (weekly), gaming/co-watching for shared activity (biweekly)—variety covers all needs
Email only for formality

questions.explanation: Platform variety strategy: Video for depth (monthly comprehensive catch-ups—closest to in-person with facial expressions and vocal tone), Messaging for frequency (weekly micro-updates maintaining presence—low effort, asynchronous), Gaming/Co-watching for shared activity (biweekly doing something together not just talking about lives—creates new shared experiences). Relying on only one platform (e.g., texting alone) provides frequency without depth, leading to relationships feeling shallow and drifting. Combination prevents boredom and covers different connection needs: frequency, intimacy, and activity.

questions.question: If you're the only person reaching out in a long-distance friendship after 4-6 weeks of consistent effort with no reciprocation, what is the evidence-based assessment?

options.text: Keep trying indefinitely—they're just busy and will reciprocate eventually
This indicates proximity was the only thing keeping friendship alive (circumstantial not foundational bond); accept relationship limitations and redirect energy to more reciprocal friendships
Demand they explain why they're not reciprocating
Ghost them completely without explanation

questions.explanation: After 4-6 weeks of consistent effort without reciprocation, this suggests: proximity was the ONLY thing keeping friendship alive (no deep bond, just convenient location), or they've genuinely let go even if you haven't. Not all friendships are meant to survive distance—if both people aren't willing to do the work of adaptation, one person can't maintain it alone. This is okay; some friendships are seasonal (Lesson 18). Accept relationship limitations without bitterness and redirect your finite energy to friendships where both people are committed. The friendships worth maintaining across distance require mutual commitment and balanced effort.

title: Maintaining Long-Distance Friendships — Connection Across Distance - Quiz
